body:
## Solution summary

Implemented on `issue-27-05a03800b7e1` and updated PR 28: https://github.com/link-foundation/gh-upload-log/pull/28

Repository uploads now fail correctly on non-zero `gh`/`git` exits, retry `gh repo create` once with a timestamp-suffixed name when the generated repo name already exists, and normalize Windows `\` paths so repository uploads don’t break on Windows. The main changes are in [src/index.js](/tmp/gh-issue-solver-1777026669578/src/index.js), with regression coverage in [test/index.test.js](/tmp/gh-issue-solver-1777026669578/test/index.test.js) and [test/cli.test.js](/tmp/gh-issue-solver-1777026669578/test/cli.test.js).

The requested evidence bundle is in [docs/case-studies/issue-27/README.md](/tmp/gh-issue-solver-1777026669578/docs/case-studies/issue-27/README.md). Local verification passed with `bun test` and `bun run check`, and GitHub Actions run `24886015374` passed Ubuntu, macOS, Windows, lint, and changeset checks. The worktree is clean and PR 28 is marked ready for review.

One residual note: the temporary private reproduction repo `konard/codex-issue27-1777026810` could not be deleted because the available `gh` token does not have the `delete_repo` scope.

---
*This summary was automatically extracted from the AI working session output.*
